Duties Description The Office of Mental Health is recruiting to fill a Senior Health Care Fiscal Analyst, G\-18, position in the Strategic Finance and Managed Care Group, Bureau of Medicaid State Plan Finance \& Data Analysis, Residential \& Rehabilitation Unit. This unit is charged with the responsibility to focus on rate setting activities necessary to plan the redesign of the mental health service infrastructure for children in New York State. The incumbent will be tasked with Duties and responsibilities including, but not limited to: • Compile current rate structures to inform rate development.• Analyze the rate sufficiency for each service (waiver and EPSDT services).• Conduct rate development for State Amendment Services and other services.o Work with programs on staffing
sections.requirements: requirements for each service.o Propose the rate and project the fiscal impact.o Review other states’ rates.o Facilitate DOH and DOB review and approval for the rate packages.• Prepare State Plan Amendment package.o Develop the federal public notice.o Produce federally required fiscal impact draft.o Draft advanced rate amendment responded to CMS RAI/other federal briefings.• Begin to draft rate and Billing guidance.o Billing logistics development (rate codes, procedure codes, modifiers, etc.)o Develop billing guidance for providers and plans.o Facilitate DOH and DOB approvals for billing guidance. The minimum qualifications required for this vacancy. A bachelor's or higher degree in accounting, auditing, business administration, economics, finance, healthcare administration, hospital administration, or public administration including or supplemented by 12 semester credit hours in accounting and/or auditing AND 6 semester additional credit hours in statistics, economics, accounting and/or auditing. sections.responsibilities: primary responsibilities will be to implement all pricing strategies and support contract marketing initiatives for respective customers including management of the daily business functions such as bids, RFI’s/RFP’s, awards, special programs, Ad Hoc, GPO’s, IDN’s under assigned commercial customers. This role will be a point of contact sales to articulate pricing recommendations and contract strategy for a variety of Fresenius Kabi customers that deliver maximum return. This is a hybrid position, and the selected candidate will be required to work onsite in the Lake Zurich, Illinois office 3 days a week. Salary Range: $85,000 \- $100,000 per year Position is eligible to participate in a bonus plan with a target of 6% of the base salary. Final pay determinations will depend on various factors, including, but not limited to Responsibilities** ==================== Pricing \& Contract Strategy * Creates and manages pricing for GPOs, IDNs, and complex key accounts; provides pricing recommendations for special orders, * bids/RFPs, and other commercial initiatives. * Collaborates with sales teams to evaluate customer usage, cross\-referencing activities, and identify opportunities throughout the * pharmaceutical portfolio; oversees and addresses customer pricing disputes as required. Analytics, Reporting \& Communication * Communicates findings, trend analyses, and recommendations verbally and in writing to management and other stakeholders; articulates risks/impacts of pricing and business\-analysis decisions while weighing multiple factors (contract strategy, pricing, and rebates). * Develops, maintains, and enhances reporting templates; prepares/distributes complex ad hoc sales and management reports; ensures data quality of all outgoing reports and analyses. * Recommends actions by analyzing and interpreting data, conducting comparative analyses, and evaluating market/competitive and customer\-specific trends; reviews business drivers using cost\-benefit analysis. * Prepares analyses for the weekly Marketing/Commercial Contracting \& Analysis update meeting and provides pricing recommendations based on results and stakeholder feedback. Bids/RFPs, Compliance \& Rebates * Compiles and maintains sales data and product information to support timely customer submissions (RFI/RFP/bids) and related analyses. * Acts as project manager and provides analytical support/guidance for GPO bid rolls and other contract marketing initiatives; participates in RFI/RFP/bid submissions. * Reviews contract compliance and recommends actions on a monthly basis; supports calculation and validation of customer rebates. Demand Planning \& Forecasting * Analyzes key KPIs (e.g., forecast accuracy) to drive demand planning improvements; partners with marketing managers and supply chain to ensure forecasts are entered/maintained in systems. * Supports new product launch/new molecule processes by keeping models and information current; supports financial forecasting, budgeting, and long\-term planning. Tools \& System * Uses SAP, Model N, Qlik, and other reporting/databases (e.g., TM1, BEX, MicroStrategy) and maintains working knowledge of CRM/CLM and RFP software platforms (e.g., Salesforce). Process Improvement * Identifies process improvement opportunities and develops/enhances tools to support team deliverables; facilitates cross\-functional and Contract Marketing process improvements. Training \& Development * Develops and maintains Contracts \& Pricing Team training library content and SOPs; provides on\-the\-job training, guidance, and quality review for Analysts/Associate Analysts. General \& Compliance * Completes all required training, including department\-specific and compliance training. * Performs other reasonable work activities as assigned by management. * All employees are responsible for ensuring the compliance to company documents, programs and activities related to the Health, Safety, Environment, Energy, and Quality Management Systems, as per your roles and responsibilities **

sections.responsibilities: Responsibilities) As our Pricing Analyst, you are the strategic bridge between Sales and Accounting. We are looking for an Operations Analyst who understands production rates and can translate field reality into winning Financial Models. * Strategic Modeling: Develop and maintain Pricing Models (labor, productivity, taxes) to ensure new business is priced accurately and profitably. * Operational Validation: Use your background as an Operations Analyst to ensure bid assumptions for staffing and equipment are realistic for the field. * Data Storytelling: Create high\-level dashboards in Tableau to monitor gross margin trends and translate raw data into actionable insights for leadership. * Bid Diplomacy: Partner with BDMs and Operations to evaluate bids and renewals, ensuring they align with margin targets and regional abilities. * Systems Integration: Seasonalize budgets into Adaptive and manage data within internal ERP systems to ensure long\-term account health.
sections.requirements: Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Finance/Accounting or related; 3\+ years in Janitorial Operations or FP\&A Expert Excel skills (modeling and logic). * Perks: 10% Bonus Potential; Military\-Friendly; Professional Development; High\-impact leadership visibility. About The Budd Group Culture The Budd Group is a leading facilities services company, but we are defined by our people. We operate with a mission to deliver high\-quality services while honoring God and helping our employees grow. When you join us, you aren't just a number; you’re a vital part of a family\-oriented team that values integrity and excellence. What You’ll Do (Key What You Bring to the Team * Expert Excel Mastery (Required): You go far beyond V\-Lookups. You must understand complex "if\-then\-else" formulas and be able to identify or fix broken calculations. * Operations Expertise: 3\+ years of
sections.experience: experience in Janitorial Operations, Building Service Contracting, or a labor\-intensive service industry. You understand P\&Ls and production rates. * Analytical Mindset: Deep understanding of the fundamentals of service\-based labor and how various "levers" (overtime, wages, turnover) impact the bottom line. * Diplomacy: You can manage "dual loyalties" between Sales (who want to win) and Accounting (who want to protect margins). * Education: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or a related field. Why Work for The Budd Group? * Stability: A debt\-free, growth\-oriented, God\-honoring company. * Support: Access to mental health and financial support resources. * Career Growth: We invest in your future with free online classes and clear advancement paths. * Impact: Your insights directly influence our competitive edge and the success of our frontline teams. The Budd Group Inc. is an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law or regulation.

sections.benefits: compensation is based on various factors, including but not limited to, the candidate’s skill set, level of benefits, and other benefits that support employee wellness, such as adoption assistance, backup family care, surrogacy benefits, employee assistance, and wellness programs. Time Off and Paid Leave\* FINRA encourages its employees to focus on their health and wellness in many ways, including through a generous time\-off program of 15 days of paid time off, 5 personal days and 9 sick days, unless otherwise required by law (all pro\-rated in the first year). Additionally, we are proud to support our communities by providing two volunteer service days (based on full\-time schedule). Other paid leave includes military leave, jury duty leave, bereavement leave, voting and election official leave for federal, state or local primary and general elections, care of a family member leave (available after 90 days of employment); and childbirth and parental leave (available after 90 days of employment). Full\-time employees receive nine paid holidays. * Based on full\-time schedule **Important Information** FINRA’s Code of Conduct imposes restrictions on employees’ investments and requires financial disclosures that are uniquely related to our role as a securities regulator. FINRA employees are required to disclose to FINRA all brokerage accounts that they maintain, and those in which they control trading or have a financial interest (including any trust account of which they are a trustee or beneficiary and all accounts of a spouse, domestic partner or minor child who lives with the employee) and to authorize their broker\-dealers to provide FINRA with duplicate statements for all of those accounts. **All of those accounts are subject to the Code’s investment and securities account restrictions, and new employees must comply with those investment** **restrictions—including** **disposing of any security issued by a company on FINRA’s Prohibited Company List or obtaining a written waiver from their Executive Vice President—by the date they begin employment with FINRA. Employees may only maintain securities accounts that must be disclosed to FINRA at one or more securities firms that provide an electronic feed (e\-feed) of data to FINRA, and must move securities accounts from other securities firms to a firm that provides an e\-feed within three months of beginning employment.** As standard practice, employees must also execute FINRA’s Employee Confidentiality and Invention Assignment Agreement without qualification or modification and comply with the company’s policy on nepotism. **Search Firm Representatives** Please be advised that FINRA is not seeking assistance or accepting unsolicited resumes from search firms for this employment opportunity. Regardless of past practice, a valid written agreement and task order must be in place before any resumes are submitted to FINRA. All resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at FINRA without a valid written agreement and task order in place will be deemed the sole property of FINRA and no fee will be paid in the event that person is hired by FINRA. **FINRA is an Equal Opportunity Employer** All qualified applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to any legally protected category, including race, color, age, national origin, ethnicity, religion, disability, genetic information, military or veteran status, sex, or any other status or classification protected by state or local law. FINRA strives to make our career site accessible to all users. sections.about: About Us:** A. Duda \& Sons, Inc. is a privately owned company, ranked \#4 on Orlando Business Journal's Top 100 Privately Held Companies list in Central Florida. Starting out as a humble celery farm in 1926, DUDA’s business operations today include Duda Farm Fresh Foods, one of the world’s leading producers and suppliers of celery and other quality fresh and fresh\-cut vegetables and citrus; The Viera Company, a highly regarded community developer with fully\-integrated real estate operations including Viera Builders, a residential home builder; as well as Duda Ranches, manager of DUDA’s agricultural operations including sod, sugarcane, citrus and cattle. **

sections.responsibilities: DUTIES** ---------------------- **THE POSITION** Under the direction of the Deputy Director, the Airport Environmental Sustainability Officer is an at\-will officer position within the Airport Department, responsible for the management of the Environmental Sustainability Division. The Airport Environmental Sustainability Officer oversees the development and implementation of environmental and sustainability compliance programs driven by local, state, and federal regulations and policies. This position serves as the environmental technical expert and is a key member of the management team in the development and execution of the Airport’s strategic objectives and key initiatives. The ideal candidate will have a considerable understanding of aviation including an in\-depth knowledge of the environmental impact of airport operations. Specific duties include:* Manages the Airport’s Storm Water Pollution Prevention Plan; * Develops short\- and long\-range strategies, policies, objectives, goals, metrics and initiatives to improve the sustainable performance of the Long Beach Airport; * Represents the Airport in City environmental committees and public forums (commissions, community working groups, stakeholders, etc.) and aviation industry organizations as they relate to sustainable airport development, operations and environmental regulatory compliance; * Implements new, innovative strategies to track performance measures, mitigate risk and respond to public environmental concerns; * Determines the allocation of resources necessary to fulfill division goals and objectives; * Represents the Airport’s interests to the federal, state agencies and other regulators regarding environmental issues; * Collaborates with fellow Airport Divisions Officers and staff on larger Airport\-related projects; * Works with airport tenants on all matters related to environmental sustainability; * Maintains relationships with airport tenants, City officials, and the public and ensures effective communication; * Performs other management functions as required. **THE IDEAL CANDIDATE** The Airport Environmental Sustainability Officer manages the Environmental Sustainability Division in the Airport Department and reports directly to the Deputy Director, who in turn reports to the Airport Director. Within the Division are 2 employees – 1 Admin Analyst III and 1 Assistant Admin Analyst II. The Airport Environmental Sustainability Officer is the Airport’s subject matter expert on environmental programs and minimizing impacts caused by airport operations. The Officer will manage assigned environmental programs at the airport for compliance with federal, state, and local rules and regulations, particularly as they pertain to air, water, soil, waste management, and recycling. They will also be responsible for producing various reports related to these programs. Work is in a public\-facing, complex, politically sensitive environment. Must be able to comfortably speak in both small and large group settings, before elected officials, governing bodies, community groups and professional organizations in response to community concerns. The Airport Environmental Sustainability Officer will envision and oversee the implementation of new or revised policies, procedures, initiatives and performance measures related to sustainability projects and programs. They will work closely with the Engineering Division to provide input related to environmental impacts from proposed projects. They will directly address related inquiries and oversee the development of new, innovative strategies and solutions to address emerging environmental issues. They must be able to identify sources of conflict and constructively mitigate causes of conflict to minimize impact on staff and stakeholders. The Airport Environmental Sustainability Officer will train and supervise the Environmental Sustainability team. They will assess the division’s regulatory
sections.requirements: qualifications, and an evaluation of internal equity within the organization. The City of Long Beach is seeking an requirements, its operational, staffing and equipment needs, and fiscal resources and work with the Finance Division to prioritize needs in light of available resources to determine and maintain the division’s budget. ** REQUIREMENTS TO FILE** ------------------------ **EDUCATION:** * A Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited college or university in Environmental Science, Environmental Engineering, Transportation Planning, or closely related field. ** REQUIREMENTS:*** A valid driver’s license, or the ability to arrange necessary and timely transportation for field travel, may be required for some assignments. **DESIRABLE QUALIFICATIONS:*** Understanding of aviation and the environmental impact of airport operations. **THE IDEAL CANDIDATE WILL POSSESS THE FOLLOWING:*** requirements. * Superior project management skills including planning, analysis, decision making, problem solving, and ability to multi\-task. * Strong written and verbal skills, comfortable speaking in both small and large group settings, before elected officials, governing bodies, community groups and professional organizations. * Ability to operate in a complex, dynamic, and politically sensitive environment. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Teams, Outlook, PowerPoint, etc.). * Valid Class C Driver License. * Ability to pass a Transportation Security Administration (TSA) mandated background check and maintain security clearance as required by law. **SELECTION PROCEDURE** ----------------------- **This recruitment will close at 11:59 P.M. (Pacific Time) on** **Friday, May 29, 2026\.** **T****o be considered, applicants must submit a resume, cover letter, and proof of education in PDF format.** Applications that fail to include all necessary documents will be considered incomplete and will not be taken into consideration. Applications will be reviewed for depth and breadth of requirements, including submission of all required attachments, will not be considered. For questions regarding this recruitment, please contact (562\) 570\-7126\. The City of Long Beach is an **Equal Opportunity Employer.** We value and encourage diversity in our workforce. The City of Long Beach is committed to creating a workplace where every employee is valued for who they are. Having our workforce reflect the diversity of our community at various levels of the organization is a continuous goal embraced by our departments, management staff, and policymakers. To support efforts of fairness and diversity, City Leadership is committed to incorporating equity and inclusion into our work by supporting staff and community partners. We are committed to promoting transparency by publishing updated demographic information for employees, including workforce diversity data and pay by race and gender. The City of Long Beach will consider qualified applicants with a criminal history pursuant to the **California Fair Chance Act**. You are not required to disclose your criminal history or participate in a background check until you receive a conditional job offer. If the City of Long Beach has concerns about a conviction that is directly related to the job after making a conditional job offer and conducting a background check, you will have the opportunity to explain the circumstances surrounding the conviction, provide evidence to mitigate concerns, or challenge the accuracy of the background report. Find out more about the Fair Chance Act by visiting https://calcivilrights.ca.gov/fair\-chance\-act. The City of Long Beach is committed to maintaining fairness and transparency in our hiring practices. We believe in providing equal opportunities to all applicants and ensuring that every applicant is evaluated based on their qualifications and merit. If you believe that you have been unjustly disqualified based on minimum qualifications for an unclassified job opportunity, you have the right to appeal the decision. Find out more about the Unclassified **Applicant Appeal Process** by visiting Applicant Appeal Process (longbeach.gov). The City of Long Beach intends to provide reasonable accommodations in accordance with the **Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990\.** If a special accommodation is desired, or if you would like to request this information in an alternative format, please call (562\) 570\-7126\. **For technical support with your governmentjobs.com application, please contact (855\) 524\-5627\.**
sections.experience: experienced, creative, innovative and collaborate individual who is committed to develop and support the Long Beach Airport. **THE COMMUNITY** Ideally located on the Pacific Ocean south of Los Angeles, adjacent to Orange County, the City of Long Beach, California (population 470,000\) is frequently described as a series of strong, diverse interwoven smaller communities within a large city. Enjoying an ideal Southern California climate, Long Beach is home to an abundance of cultural and recreational options. The Long Beach Convention Center, Aquarium of the Pacific, Queen Mary, and the annual Acura Grand Prix of Long Beach, plus a wide variety of other attractions (two historic ranchos, three marinas, and five golf courses), serve to draw 6\.5 million visitors a year. The City is also home to California State University, Long Beach, and Long Beach City College. Cal State Long Beach is the second largest university in the state and was recently ranked the No. 3 best–value public college in the nation. Covering approximately 50 square miles, Long Beach is supported by a wide mix of industries with education, health and social services, manufacturing, retail trade, and professional services comprising the highest representation. Known for its livable and desirable neighborhoods, Long Beach was named by America’s Promise Alliance as one of the 100 Best Communities for Young People two years in a row. While it offers all the amenities of a large metropolis, many say Long Beach offers the added benefit of having maintained a strong sense of community and cohesiveness despite its growth. A superb climate, quality schools, a vibrant downtown, and a wide variety of neighborhoods help make Long Beach one of the most livable communities in the country. **CITY GOVERNMENT** Long Beach, a charter city formed in 1897, is governed by nine City Council Members elected by district and a city\-wide elected Mayor. Other elected officials include the City Attorney, City Auditor, and City Prosecutor. The elected officials are elected to staggered four\-year terms. The City Council appoints the City Manager, City Clerk and Director of Police Oversight. The City Manager is responsible for the efficient administration of all City departments, excluding those under the direction of a separately elected official, Board or Commission. Long Beach is one of only three cities in California with its own Health Department and one of the very few municipalities with its own Utilities Department (Water and Gas) and Energy Resources Departments. The City is supported by a FY2025 total budget of approximately $3\.6 billion with a General Fund budget of $752 million. More than 6,000 full and part\-time employees support municipal operations with the vast majority being represented by eleven employee associations. To learn more about the City of Long Beach, go to: www.longbeach.gov. **THE DEPARTMENT** Recognized as America's Coolest Airport, LGB offers a unique blend of convenience, efficiency, and a relaxed atmosphere that sets it apart from larger, busier airports. Passengers appreciate shorter lines, quicker security checks, and easy access to gates, making travel less stressful and more enjoyable. Central to the Los Angeles and Orange County areas, with an unparalleled passenger experience, LGB provides leisure travelers with the perfect gateway to Southern California's attractions while providing valuable time savings to business travelers. In a recent ranking by *The Washington Post,* LGB was recognized as the second\-best airport in the United States, based on factors such as accessibility, ease of navigation, architectural appeal, amenities, and unique features. Long Beach Airport (LGB) is classified as a small hub airport in the National Plan of Integrated Airport Systems and serves approximately four million passengers annually. Owned and operated by the City of Long Beach, LGB was established in 1923 and is one of the oldest municipal airports in the United States. Known as the Coolest Airport in America, LGB is a friendly alternative to other Southern California airports, offering preferred flight schedules, great airlines and destinations and overall accessibility. The Airport is committed to delivering an Outstanding Customer Experience in an intelligent. ecofriendly, safe, and efficient manner while being respectful of our employees and the community that we serve. We also strive to be a major driver in the economic vitality of Long Beach. Through Organizational Excellence, we seek to recruit, develop, and retain talented staff in an organization structured for success. Long Beach Airport has a rich history of contributions to our nation's leadership in aviation. Earl Daugherty opened one of the nation's first flight schools in Long Beach in 1919\. Charles Lindbergh and Amelia Earhart flew from Long Beach. Once home to McDonnell Douglas (now Boeing), many commercial aircraft including the DC\-8, DC\-9/MD\-80, DC\-10/MD\-11, and C\-17 were manufactured in Long Beach. Approximately 180 businesses reside on Airport property and are responsible for roughly 18,000 jobs. Some of these include AAA. Aero Business Center, Atlantic Aviation, Long Beach Marriott Hotel, DeVry Institute of Technology, DHL, Epson America, SCAN Health Plan, Signature @ Aeroplex, Skylinks Golf Course, and United Parcel Service. Companies located near the Airport, such as JetZero and Ampaire are revolutionizing the aerospace industry and leading the way forward with sustainable aircraft designs. The future of space travel is being shaped by local companies including Relativity Space, Rocket Labs, VAST and others. Along with our partners, LGB is a key contributor to the economic vitality of the region. The Airport recently completed Phase II of the Terminal Area Improvements Project along with significant roadway improvements. New courses and boarding lounges were opened in 2012\. These facilities will soon undergo a refresh in preparation for the 2028 Olympics. A new Ticketing Facility featuring Common Use Passenger Processing was opened in 2022\. A new Baggage Claim Facility was opened in 2023 and the renovated Historic Terminal, originally opened in 1941, reopened in 2025\. The Airport Department is governed by the City Council; the Airport Director is appointed by the City Manager and is responsible for overall airport activities. The Director is supported by a Deputy Director, Manager of Airport Operations and Facilities and Manager of Finance and Administration. The Airport Department employs approximately 125 staff and has an annual operating budget of approximately $28 million. **EXAMPLES OF EXPERIENCE:*** Four (4\) years of progressively responsible sustainability, environmental compliance, planning, and/or related work, including two (2\) years in a supervisory or lead capacity, and at least two (2\) years of aviation industry experience. + A combination of education/ experience can be substituted for the required education on a year for year basis. **ADDITIONAL Experience reviewing local, state and federal environmental regulations and experience, and for level and relatedness of education. The most qualified candidates will be invited to participate in further selection procedures. The selected candidate may be required to go through a background check and a physical examination prior to appointment.Applicants who do not meet the minimum

sections.experience: experienced data analyst to bring strong problem\-solving and analytical skills to a leading charter management organization. KIPP Texas is committed to using data to drive academic, financial, instructional, operational and talent improvement and decision\-making. The Data Analyst is critical to KIPP Texas achieving this work. This individual must be as much a people person as a numbers person \- collaboration and building strong cross\-functional partnerships are necessary to be successful in this role. Note: The team’s existing BI tool is Tableau and we will be transitioning to Power BI by January 2027\. This position reports to the Senior Director, Analytics, Research \& Evaluation and follows a hybrid schedule (coming to our SSP office in Houston 3 days a week; and work from home 2 days a week). This role is expected to travel to other regions and/or sites 5%\-10% of the year. **ROLE experience, with a preference for candidates who have worked in the public sector or similar mission\-driven environments. **COMPETENCY AND SKILLS:** * Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Power BI, including expertise in report design, data modeling (star schema), and development of complex calculated measures using DAX and Power Query (M). * Mastery of SQL for complex querying, data extraction, and deep understanding of relational databases and data warehousing concepts (e.g., normalization, schema design). * Experience working within the Azure Data Ecosystem and an understanding of ETL/ELT processes to move and transform data for analytics. * Proficiency with Tableau is preferred due to the ongoing transition/migration project. * Strong background in statistical analysis required, with proficiency in Python or R * Mastery of Microsoft Excel * Superior quantitative and technical skills * Proven ability to work with large, disparate datasets, ensuring data cleaning, validation, and manipulation are rigorous and efficient. * Strong written and oral communication skills, specifically the ability to translate complex data findings and technical Experience working with a team and collaborating effectively with cross\-functional stakeholders (e.g., Finance, Academics, School Leaders) **Physical

sections.experience: experienced data analyst to bring strong problem\-solving and analytical skills to a leading charter management organization. KIPP Texas is committed to using data to drive academic, financial, instructional, operational and talent improvement and decision\-making. The Data Analyst is critical to KIPP Texas achieving this work. This individual must be as much a people person as a numbers person \- collaboration and building strong cross\-functional partnerships are necessary to be successful in this role. Note: The team’s existing BI tool is Tableau and we will be transitioning to Power BI by January 2027\. This position reports to the Senior Director, Analytics, Research \& Evaluation and follows a hybrid schedule (coming to our SSP office in Houston 3 days a week; and work from home 2 days a week). This role is expected to travel to other regions and/or sites 5%\-10% of the year. **ROLE experience, with a preference for candidates who have worked in the public sector or similar mission\-driven environments. **COMPETENCY AND SKILLS:** * Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Power BI, including expertise in report design, data modeling (star schema), and development of complex calculated measures using DAX and Power Query (M). * Mastery of SQL for complex querying, data extraction, and deep understanding of relational databases and data warehousing concepts (e.g., normalization, schema design). * Experience working within the Azure Data Ecosystem and an understanding of ETL/ELT processes to move and transform data for analytics. * Proficiency with Tableau is preferred due to the ongoing transition/migration project. * Strong background in statistical analysis required, with proficiency in Python or R * Mastery of Microsoft Excel * Superior quantitative and technical skills * Proven ability to work with large, disparate datasets, ensuring data cleaning, validation, and manipulation are rigorous and efficient. * Strong written and oral communication skills, specifically the ability to translate complex data findings and technical Experience working with a team and collaborating effectively with cross\-functional stakeholders (e.g., Finance, Academics, School Leaders) **Physical

sections.benefits: Benefits: **Dental insurance \| Health insurance \| Vision insurance \| Paid time off \| HSA \& FSA \| Retirement plan** Resume and Cover Letter are mandatory to apply for any position. \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* **Payroll Analyst, Senior** **SUMMARY** ----------- Under general supervision, perform general ledger analysis of payroll liability accounts and third party vendors; provide technical and functional support for all payroll operations including initiating and reviewing reports to ensure data integrity and accuracy of the following: payroll simulations, wage reports, bank and vendor transactions; responsible for payroll taxes, tax audit compliance and tax updates; support the Payroll Manager in oversight of successful Payroll Department operations. **ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS** \-*Essential functions, as defined under the Americans with Disabilities Act, may include any of the following representative benefits for accuracy. Calculate and verify retroactive salary data. Perform random audits and data analysis testing on payroll time entries. * Assist Payroll Manager with quarterly and annual tax reports including 941’s and W\-2’s. Pay Biweekly Federal and State taxes. Assist in coordinating year end balancing. * Collaborate with Finance team to analyze payroll processes and systems to identify inefficiencies and research alternatives to resolve critical payroll issues. * In collaboration with internal departments, prepare, balance, and submit 3rd party vendor files. * Collect payroll data and information for statistical analysis. Responsible for ongoing analysis of payroll data for budgeting. Create ad hoc reports as requested for research data projects. * Participates in and/or leads various special projects as identified by the payroll manager in support of Human Resource and Finance initiatives. * Assist the payroll manager in oversight of daily payroll operations. Assist manager on annual financial audits, including preparation of payroll liability account reconciliations. * Effectively communicates with other district departments, vendors, and government agencies in regard to payroll related operations. * Provide customer services; respond to requests for information, and attempt to resolve the requests by researching files and records within the scope of authority; update, retrieve and release information according to procedures; refer matters requiring policy interpretation to supervisor for resolution. * Demonstrate courteous and cooperative behavior when interacting with students, clients, visitors, and MPS staff; act in a manner that promotes a harmonious and effective workplace environment * Enthusiastically promotes the Superintendent’s goals and priorities in compliance with all policies and procedures. * Maintain absolute confidentiality of work\-related issues, records and MPS information. * Other benefits. * Accounting and budgeting principles and methods for public sector payroll accounting. * Payroll and benefits accounting rules and regulations, including union contracts and multiple pension plans. * Specialized MPS and state agency accounting software applications. * Principles of record keeping and records management. * Personal computers utilizing standard and specialized software applications. **Skill in:** * Interpreting and applying MPS policies, and Federal, state and local laws governing payroll, taxes, and benefits. * Maintaining payroll documentation, verifying, and reconciling payroll information and reports. * Meeting deadlines and strict time frames for payroll processing. * Correcting and updating financial information systems and generating reports. * Maintaining accurate accounting records, and identifying and reconciling errors. * Performing mathematical calculations with skill and accuracy. * Reviewing and correcting mathematical calculations and computations. * Operating a personal computer using specialized software, and entering information into a computer system with speed and accuracy. * Ability to work independently, with minimal supervision, in a technical environment with interlinked and changing priorities. * Establishing and maintaining effective working relationships with co\-workers. * Effective verbal and written communication. **LICENSE AND CERTIFICATION

sections.responsibilities: Responsibilities** -------------------- * Work with Lender to collect financial and related data in order to determine the general credit worthiness of prospects and customers and determine the merits of the specific loan request * Prepare the loan submission form, addendums, and other components of the loan submission package for both new and maturing loans * Otherwise manage all aspects of assigned portfolio * Obtain and retain product knowledge * Support the collection of past due loans * Support the clearing of financial statement and documentation exceptions * Direct financial information to credit analysts for spreading and analysis * Monitor new developments in lending, compliance with loan policies, and compliance with appropriate regulations * Assist in preparation of various reports and submissions to credit administration and loan review * Participate with Lender in ongoing marketing to include making direct sales calls to prospects and customers * Assume ownership of assigned loans with little or no direct supervision * May manage a team of Portfolio Managers which includes but not limited to hiring, training, coaching, personnel reviews, and disciplinary action * Perform other related duties as assigned ** duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to stand or sit; kneel, stoop, or squat; use hands or fingers to handle or feel objects, tools or controls; reach with hands and arms, and talk or hear. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and the ability to focus. **Work Environment** The Bank’s professional working environment requires employees to communicate effectively, both verbally and in writing. Employees must demonstrate strong interpersonal skills when working closely with internal business partners and external clients. Employees may be exposed to confidential and propriety information within the working environment, therefore, must uphold confidentiality at all times. Due to the possibility of being exposed to high risk situations (i.e. robbery), detailed instructions and procedures are required to be followed at all times to safeguard the Bank’s employees, customers, and assets. sections.responsibilities: DUTIES:** * **Strategic Financial Leadership \& Capital Planning:** Architects and leads the development of the citywide capital budget and multi\-year financial plan, ensuring fiscal strategies align with City Council priorities, community goals, and long\-term sustainability initiatives. Manages the full CIP process, including the preparation of the City's comprehensive 5\-year Capital Improvement Plan. * **Revenue Forecasting \& Fee Analysis:** Develops sophisticated revenue models that account for the unique volatility of a resort economy—including sales tax, property tax, and fees—to ensure the city maintains healthy reserves and financial resiliency. Prepares long\-term revenue and expenditure projections and ensures departmental fees are accurate, compliant with state regulations, and updated as needed. * **Team Mentorship and Oversight:** Directs and empowers the budget team, providing high\-level guidance to the Assistant Budget Director and Senior Analyst to ensure rigorous departmental budget reviews, accurate forecasting, and a culture of continuous professional development. * **Debt Management \& Financial Advisory Coordination:** Supports the City’s Treasurer with debt issuance, including research, financial analysis, reporting, and coordination with City Council and rating agencies. Plays a key role in maintaining and improving the City’s credit rating. Oversee contracts with Bond Counsel and the Municipal Financial Advisor. * **Long\-Term Financial Planning \& Fund Analysis:** Serves as a strategic advisor to Department Heads and Executive Leadership in developing and maintaining long\-range financial plans for all major City funds. Builds and updates financial models to forecast future revenues, expenditures, and capital needs. * **Procurement Oversight \& Strategy:** Supervises the Procurement Manager and provides strategic oversight of the City’s procurement program in conjunction with the City Attorney’s Office. Ensures the effective development, issuance, and execution of procurement documents — including Requests for Proposals (RFPs), Requests for duties as assigned. **MINIMUM duties, responsibilities, and
sections.requirements: Qualifications (RFQs), and Invitations to Bid — to secure goods and services incompliance with City policies and applicable laws. * **Public Engagement and Transparency** Champion fiscal transparency by translating complex financial data into accessible presentations and reports for the City Council and the public, fostering community trust during public meetings. * Other QUALIFICATIONS:** * Bachelor’s degree in finance or related field * 5\+ years demonstrated supervisory QUALIFICATIONS:** * Master of Public Administration (MPA) or related master’s level degree. * 10\+ years of demonstrated process or project management skills required by personnel so classified. All requirements are subject to possible modification to reasonably accommodate individuals with disabilities.

We are committed to maintaining a diverse workforce and providing employment opportunities to veterans and individuals who have a disability. **To learn more about the Department of Revenue’s excellent array of benefits, including career training, tuition waivers, paid vacations, insurance, and retirement programs, visit our** **website****.** **JOB SUMMARY:**The Florida Department of Revenue’s Information Services Program (ISP) is seeking a highly organized and analytical professional to serve as an Operations Analyst II supporting procurement, contract administration, and operational monitoring activities. This position provides comprehensive support across procurement and contract operations, including purchasing coordination, contract lifecycle administration, data tracking, and operational reporting. The incumbent works within established policies and procedures while collaborating with contract managers, procurement staff, program areas, and financial teams to ensure procurement activities are executed accurately, efficiently, and in compliance with state regulations and Department standards. The role also supports data analysis, reporting, and process improvement initiatives by maintaining tracking systems, monitoring procurement performance, and identifying opportunities to enhance operational efficiency across procurement and contract management processes. **MINIMUM BENEFITS:** Benefits include, but are not limited to, health insurance, life insurance, tuition waivers, paid sick and personal leave, paid parental leave, 10 paid holidays annually, retirement savings, and vision and dental insurance. **ADDITIONAL INFORMATION YOU NEED TO KNOW** **CONTACT INFORMATION:**Regenia Walker, (850\) 717\-7346, Regenia.Walker@floridarevenue.com. **SCREENING DISCLAIMER:**Your responses to qualifying questions must be verifiable by skills and/or

sections.responsibilities: Responsibilities In collaboration with Product, Sales, and Finance teams, help influence and guide global pricing policy and strategy Develop and provide ongoing support for Product team pricing models, leveraging business guardrails to achieve desired outcomes, and equip Sales and Product with pricing tools and guidance throughout the selling cycle Perform product portfolio analysis on a regular cadence to drive actionable insights regarding product and customer profitability, and evaluate margin improvement opportunities Perform market analyses using internal and customer/industry data to analyze growth and pricing trends by customer, product, market segment, competitive pricing, and customer willingness to pay to inform pricing decisions Identify trends and provide meaningful insights to Product, Sales, and Finance teams to drive pricing, promotional opportunities, and better forecast accuracy Act as a key contributor in budget and forecast activities, responsible for forecasting and explaining pricing impacts each year, and ensure alignment with revenue and profitability targets Establish pricing governance processes, approval workflows, and guardrails to ensure consistency and compliance Establish processes for annual price reviews and mid\-year pricing actions, including list price adjustments and discounting Support benchmarking of pricing levels and margins across product families (good/better/best), manufacturing locations, and broader organizational guardrails Perform detailed margin and cost analyses, including cost\-plus modeling, value\-based pricing, and scenario modeling Understand customer pricing agreements, including header discounts, product\-specific and volume\-based rebates, and other price concessions; support Sales and Sales Operations in customer pricing management Improve pricing data quality, visibility, and reporting across the organization, including developing KPIs and pricing insights to support leadership decision\-making Seek opportunities to leverage Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to improve and optimize pricing processes
sections.requirements: Qualifications BA/BS in Finance, Economics, Marketing, or related field preferred 5\+ years of relevant professional
sections.experience: experience in manufacturing and product costing, data analytics and reporting, strategic pricing analysis, market and POS data analysis, or commercial analysis Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with internal partners Dynamic, self\-starter who can work independently Advanced Microsoft Excel skills Experience with SAP and Business Intelligence tools a plus Willingness and ability to travel up to 5% of the time The salary range is $95,000 USD up to $130,000 based on skills and experience. This position is eligible for an annual corporate bonus based on company and individual performance. About SRAM SRAM LLC is one of the world's largest suppliers of components to the bike industry. Established in Chicago in 1987, SRAM continues to promote cycling through its products, its advocacy, and its employees who are dedicated to improving the cycling experience. Today, SRAM employs more than 5,000\+ employees, in 20\+ locations, across 10\+ countries. SRAM is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

sections.responsibilities: Responsibilities: * Work with various functions across Eaton to implement Global Payables projects to ensure timely execution based on approved project plans. * Lead the execution of the supplier add/change processes for key projects. * Serve as a Global Payables SME and provide inputs as an expert in Global Payables processes to support project work. * Conduct Global Payables trainings and provide ongoing support. * Complete and/or coordinate upgrade testing for various Global Payables applications. * Document and share best practices and lessons learned related to Global Payables projects. * Develop process knowledge as it relates to Global Payables applications. * Support change management activities within project implementations. * Forecasts and reports time spent working on projects to support Global Payables budget. **
sections.requirements: qualifications, as well as market and business considerations. **What you’ll do:** ------------------- We are currently seeking an Senior Analyst Global Payables. This position is based at our North American HQ in Beachwood, OH with hybrid work available. Position Overview: The role supports the Global Payables processes and procedures for Eaton's Oracle or SAP based systems. This role will be a fundamental project team member, which will help achieve the successful implementations of the approved project pipeline for the Global Payables workstream annually. Job Qualifications:** ------------------- Required (Basic) Qualifications: * Bachelor’s degree from an accredited institution required. * Minimum of 3 years working in Accounting or Finance. * Relocation assistance is not available for this position. Only candidates currently located within a 50 mile radius of Beachwood, OH will be considered. Active Duty Military Service member candidates are exempt from the geographical area limitation. * Must be able to work in the United States without corporate sponsorship now and within the future. **Skills:** ----------- Position Criteria: * Strategic and process improvement mindset. * Excellent problem solving and written and oral communication skills. * Detail\-oriented with effective organization and time management skills. * Highly motivated, highly analytical and self\-starter. * Ability to work with a global team. * 10% travel required All positions may require participation in video and in\-person interviews as part of the hiring process. All candidates will be evaluated based on job\-related competencies, and all candidates’ privacy rights and data security will be protected in accordance with applicable laws. Employment decisions are based upon job\-related reasons regardless of an applicant's race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability, marital status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other status protected by law. Eaton believes in second chance employment. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction history will be considered regardless of their arrest or conviction history, consistent with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the California Fair Chance Act and other local laws. You do not need to disclose your conviction history or participate in a background check until a conditional job offer is made to you. After making a conditional offer and running a background check, if Eaton is concerned about conviction that is directly related to the job, you will be given the chance to explain the circumstances surrounding the conviction, provide mitigating evidence, or challenge the accuracy of the background report. To request a disability\-related reasonable accommodation to assist you in your job search, application, or interview process, please call us at 1\-800\-836\-6345 to discuss your specific need. sections.responsibilities: Responsibilities** **Land Documentation \& Agreement Management** * Prepare, review, and populate lease option agreements, easements, and related land contracts * Coordinate with internal teams and external legal counsel on Memoranda of Lease (MOLs) and other recordable instruments * Assemble, distribute, and track execution packages, ensuring timely completion and accuracy * Maintain strict version control and audit trails across all land agreements **Document Control \& Data Management** * Maintain and continuously update the master land and lease database, ensuring data accuracy and completeness * Track parcel ownership, acreage, agreement status, and key obligations across project portfolios * Implement and enforce document management best practices, including naming conventions, storage protocols, and access controls * Serve as the central point of control for all executed land documents and metadata **Land Systems Implementation \& Management** * Lead or support implementation of land management systems (e.g., *Pandell, Quorum, or similar platforms*) * Configure and maintain system fields, workflows, and reporting structures to align with development needs * Migrate legacy data from spreadsheets and disparate sources into centralized systems, ensuring data integrity and consistency * Develop and maintain standardized reporting dashboards (acreage secured, payment status, title status, etc.) * Train internal users (landmen, development, accounting) on system usage and best practices * Continuously improve system functionality and processes to support scalability and portfolio growth **Landowner \& Payment Administration** * Collect and verify landowner documentation, including: * + W\-9s + ACH/payment instructions + Vesting deeds and ownership documentation * Validate agreement terms (acreage, rates, payment triggers) prior to payment processing * Coordinate closely with Accounting to ensure timely, accurate, and compliant payments * Track option payments, renewals, and extension deadlines, ensuring no lapse in site control **Recording \& Title Coordination** * Coordinate recording of documents with county recorders and applicable jurisdictions * Track recording status and confirm receipt of recording numbers and proper indexing * Order and manage title reports and runsheets, ensuring timely delivery to stakeholders * Track and help drive resolution of title curative items in coordination with landmen and legal **Cross\-Functional Support** * Partner closely with: * + Land acquisition team (landmen) + Legal counsel + Development and project management teams + Accounting and finance * Support project diligence efforts by ensuring clean, organized, and audit\-ready land records * Identify and implement process improvements to enhance efficiency, visibility, and risk mitigation **
sections.requirements: Qualifications** **
sections.experience: Experience** * 5\+ years of experience in land administration, real estate, title, paralegal, or document control roles * Experience supporting energy, infrastructure, or oil \& gas development projects strongly preferred * Demonstrated experience implementing or managing land systems (e.g., Pandell, Quorum, or similar) **Technical Skills** * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel (data tracking, reconciliation, reporting) * Experience with land management systems and document control platforms * Working knowledge of: * + Land records and title documentation + Legal instruments (leases, easements, memoranda) + County recording processes **Key Competencies** * Exceptional attention to detail and data accuracy * Strong systems thinker with ability to design scalable workflows * Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously * Effective communicator across technical and non\-technical teams * Proactive, solutions\-oriented mindset with strong ownership and accountability **Education** * Bachelor's degree in Business, Real Estate, Paralegal Studies, or related field preferred **Why Join Quantica Infrastructure** * Opportunity to support large\-scale, high\-impact energy and infrastructure projects * Play a key role in building scalable land systems and processes from the ground up * Collaborative, entrepreneurial environment with strong growth potential * Competitive

sections.responsibilities: Responsibilities** **Essential Functions:** Solution Architecture Design:* Design and implement OpenText Extended ECM solutions that integrate with SAP systems, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and SAP Business Suite. * Architect solutions that bridge structured SAP data with unstructured content to create a unified view of business objects (e.g., customers, vendors, purchase orders). * Develop custom workspaces within OpenText ECM tailored to SAP use cases, such as procurement files, employee records, or asset lifecycle management. SAP Integration Expertise:* Integrate OpenText Extended ECM with SAP using integration methods such as SAP ArchiveLink, CMIS, SAP Fiori, and other APIs or middleware solutions. * Design and implement data and document archiving strategies for SAP environments, ensuring efficient management of unstructured content linked to SAP transactions. * Collaborate with SAP teams to ensure seamless integration and content management across business processes like finance, HR, procurement, and engineering. Content Management Optimization:* Manage the lifecycle of unstructured content (documents, emails, images) and ensure proper metadata management, classification, and governance. * Implement automated workflows to streamline business processes, such as invoice management, HR onboarding, or engineering document control. * Apply records management standards and ensure compliance with regulations (e.g., DoD 5015\.2, GDPR) within SAP and OpenText systems. Cloud and Deployment Expertise:* Lead the deployment of OpenText Extended ECM in various environments (cloud, on\-premises, hybrid) and manage integration with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud. * Align ECM solutions with SAP's RISE or GROW programs to support SaaS\-based content services and modern business operations. Customization and Automation:* Use programming skills in languages like Java, JavaScript, or ABAP to create custom solutions, automate workflows, and integrate systems. * Implement AI\-powered features such as OpenText Content Aviator or SAP Joule to enhance automation, analytics, and document management capabilities. Project Implementation and Delivery:* Lead blueprinting, architecture design, installation, configuration, and testing of OpenText Extended ECM solutions for SAP projects. * Apply methodologies like OpenText’s Fastrack or similar frameworks to deliver projects on time, within budget, and with high quality. Data Migration and Upgrades:* Manage data migration strategies to move legacy ECM systems into OpenText Extended ECM while ensuring compatibility with SAP updates and new versions. Leadership and Collaboration:* Lead and mentor implementation teams, coordinating with OpenText Professional Services and cross\-functional teams, including SAP Basis, developers, and business analysts. * Work closely with business stakeholders to identify content management needs and design solutions that enhance operational efficiency and compliance. ### **Skills, Knowledge, and duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice. Qellus, LLC is an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, non\-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status or any other basis covered by appropriate law. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants and employees. **Work Authorization

OpenText Extended ECM and its integration with SAP systems, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and SAP Business Suite. * Strong knowledge of SAP integration methods (e.g., SAP ArchiveLink, CMIS, SAP Fiori) and data/document archiving strategies. * Proficiency in content management best practices, metadata management, and compliance standards. * Experience with cloud deployments (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and hybrid environments. * Programming skills in Java, JavaScript, ABAP, or experience with low\-code/no\-code configuration within OpenText tools **Functional Skills:*** Ability to design ECM solutions that map SAP business processes to content management needs across various business functions (e.g., finance, HR, procurement, plant maintenance, etc.). * Expertise in designing solutions for key SAP business processes, FI, PM, PS, MM, etc. * Strong knowledge of solution design and implementation frameworks like OpenText Fastrack. **Soft Skills:*** Excellent problem\-solving skills, with the ability to troubleshoot integration issues and optimize system performance. * Strong communication skills, with the ability to collaborate with business users, SAP teams, and stakeholders to define experience leading implementation teams and coordinating cross\-functional efforts. * Adaptability to evolving SAP and OpenText technologies, including cloud and sections.responsibilities: Responsibilities: **Job Summary:** The Procurement Systems job family is responsible for the accurate setup, maintenance, reporting and financial governance of retail products across enterprise systems with a focus on indirect/non\-resale products. Ensuring data integrity and compliance by coordinating product configurations, cost structures, and supplier relationships, while supporting downstream processes including inventory, accounting, and procurement. **Key Responsibilities \& Essential Functions:** * Processes routine transactions to ensure system and data integrity. * Monitor indirect product lifecycle indicators and flag items with low usage or approaching obsolescence for review. * Support indirect cost maintenance by validating basic cost inputs and assisting in the resolution of purchase order discrepancies. * Support resolving straightforward issues related to indirect product setup, cost accuracy, and system configurations. * Assists/collaborate with others to identify, document, analyze and facilitate implementation of accounting systems and applications, and communicate solutions to meet business needs. * Assists in analysis of probable impact of recommendations / solutions for downstream product impacts such as tax classification, general ledger coding, and unit conversions. * Assists project teams in delivery of applications, systems, processes, and services in agreed\-upon timeframes; assists in testing. * Assist with post\-implementation analysis * Supports preparation of reports, graphs and charts to illustrate and communicate key performance indicators. * Supports preparation of audits for product, cost, and supplier data and system user management/access to ensure compliance with internal controls and segregation of duties. * Focus on gaining knowledge of end\-to\-end processes and understanding system interdependencies to provide solution recommendations. *The responsibilities and essential functions outlined above describe the general nature and level of work assigned to this position. This is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities, and Duties and responsibilities may be modified at any time based on business needs. Employees may be required to perform other job\-related tasks as requested by their supervisor, subject to reasonable accommodations.* **

Indiana University Health is unlike any other healthcare system, and we're looking for team members who share the things that matter most to us. IU Health leaders play a crucial role in advancing the mission of our organization and inspiring teams to make a meaningful difference in the lives of patients. As one of Indiana’s largest employers and the most comprehensive medical system in the state, our vision is ***to lead the transformation of healthcare through quality, innovation and education, and make Indiana one*** ***of the nation’s healthiest states.*** IU Health is the largest health system in Indiana with nearly 40,000 team members, 15 hospitals, and $8\.64 billion in operating revenue. The system’s programs in cancer, cardiovascular, neuroscience, orthopedics, pediatrics and transplants have received national recognition for quality patient care. We deliver solutions that help our nation and allies defend freedoms and deter aggression, creating a safer, more secure world. Join us and help shape the future of aerospace and defense. At Raytheon Missiles \& Defense, you have the opportunity to try new things and make a bigger difference across a broader end\-to\-end solution, a richer technology and product set, an expanded range of disciplines, a growing global footprint and a more diverse team of colleagues and customers. Fresh thinking and possibilities are forged in times of change, and you will be on the front lines as we trailblaze new approaches, push the boundaries of innovation and chart a course to a tomorrow you can be proud to have a hand in creating. This opening is for the Finance Lead of the Integrated Ship Solutions \& Support (IS3\) programs within the Ship Systems Integration (SSI) portfolio, part of the Naval Systems \& Sustainment (NSS) product area and the Naval Power mission area. This role will support IS3 program leadership and oversee the financial scope of the Polar Security Cutter, Lifecycle and TAO/ESB contracts as well as proposal and capture opportunities. The Finance Lead will perform internal earned value management, IPDS gate activities, quarterly Estimates at Compete (EACs), funding analysis, balance sheet management, staffing analysis, and other financial control activities. This position is also responsible for the monthly forecasting and risk \& opportunity management of IS3 financials. They are a critical contributor to developing the monthly Operational Workforce Plan (OWP), Annual Operating Plan (AOP), and Long Range Plan (LRP). This position is a key member of the IS3 Leadership Team and will need to be able to work across a matrixed organization to effectively analyze and message financial results. The role of IS3 Finance Lead offers a terrific opportunity to own the complete financial process of a group of programs spanning capture, execution, and closeout. The role provides the opportunity to increase exposure to multiple facets of not only the SSI, NSS, and Naval Power finance organization, but also Program and Functional leadership teams. ** experience and minimum 8 years prior relevant experience, or An Advanced Degree in a related field and minimum 5 years experience. * Experience with APEX/SAP Managed Programs finance module \& Business Warehouse or similar system. * Experience in program cost schedule and control environment * Experience with Microsoft Office Suite ** experienced professional, we offer a total rewards package that goes above and beyond with experience levels. sections.about: About the Role** As Inventory Manager, you will lead strategic inventory initiatives across global operations, leveraging advanced analytics, automation, and machine learning to optimize inventory levels, reduce waste, and improve supply chain responsiveness. You will collaborate cross\-functionally with Sales, Operations, Product Management, and Finance to drive data\-informed decisions and continuous improvement in inventory governance. **

sections.responsibilities: Duties** **60% Financial Oversight of Unrestricted Funds** * Supports the Senior Assistant Dean of F\&A with financial planning and analysis, commitments tracking, and budget scenario forecasting. * Serves as a senior resource for overseeing the college’s annual operating budget of unrestricted funds including operating accounts, start\-up funds, etc. Monitors financial projects and activity to ensure appropriate use of funds, alignment with strategic plans, F\&A team priorities, and compliance with university and college policies and procedures. Works closely with college leadership and senior financial staff to analyze balances, expenditures, and projections, while supporting a range of budgetary and financial projects or initiatives. * Serves as a senior resource for resolving complex financial issues related to unrestricted funds. Serves as the College’s primary training resource for financial matters by designing and delivering on demand and/or tailored training for financial and business operations staff; develops and maintains standard operating procedures (SOPs) for financial monitoring, account reconciliation, budget projections, financial reporting, and year\-end closeout; creates and maintains supporting documentation (checklists, templates, etc.) to promote consistency and best practices across departments. Assists in the development and maintenance of standardized tools, reports, and best practices to support financial monitoring and decision\-making. * Serves as cost center manager as deemed necessary. Reconciles college\-level accounts ensuring expenditures are appropriate and an approved college commitment. Conducts regular financial reviews and variance analyses; identifies risks, trends, or deficits and develops mitigation strategies. * Provides high\-level support in the college’s fiscal year\-end closeout activities, including reviewing account balances and transactions for accuracy and allowability; ensuring timely processing of financial transactions, commitments, and corrections; and coordinating with central administrative offices to meet fiscal year deadlines. * Manages the college’s summer budget and serves as the primary financial lead, working closely with college leadership as well as HR and senior financial staff. Responsibilities include establishing and loading the budget, verifying salary and enrollment data, tracking profit/loss, and ensuring overall financial accuracy. Serves as cost center manager and effort certification reviewer for all summer budget accounts. * Serves as the financial lead for the college’s summer research project, collaborating directly with college leadership and HR and senior financial staff. Responsibilities include compiling the college’s file for EIB submission, and resolving discrepancies with LSU HRM and departments, and ensuring compliance with reporting responsibilities. * Strengthens the College’s post\-award environment by leading college\-level financial training and knowledge\-sharing efforts and disseminating post\-award communication to appropriate parties. Develops and maintains college\-level tools and standards (SOPs, checklists, templates, and best practice guidance) that enable departments to manage grants effectively and independently. Ensures guidelines and training to promote a culture of improving accuracy, constancy, and compliance while reducing audit exposure. Recommends process improvements that strengthen the College’s financial control environment and reputation. * Serves as Dean’s Office grant analyst. * Interprets and applies university, state, and sponsor financial policies across key ledger accounts, including personnel (summer salary, graduate assistants, student workers), equipment, travel, procurement, scholarships, and participant support costs. Maintains expert\-level knowledge of LSU financial systems, LSU financial and business policies and SOPs across all fund and account types (unrestricted, restricted, private; grants, start\-up, scholarships, operating). **5% Other** * Performs other duties and/or supports special financial projects and initiatives as assigned by the Senior Assistant Dean of F\&A. **Minimum
sections.requirements: requirements. * Partners with the F\&A team member(s) overseeing restricted and private funds to understand the college’s portfolio including ICR, LSU Online, technology transfer, scholarships, discretionary funds, etc. * Monitors financial reports and recommends enhancements to improve the accuracy, clarity, and usefulness of financial data reporting. **35% Financial Advising \& Training (Post\-Award)** * Provides high\-level, college\-wide financial oversight and analysis of a distinct portfolio of sponsored projects to inform leadership of financial trends, emerging risk, and systemic gaps, while preserving departmental responsibility for day\-to\-day grant management. Serves as a strategic financial advisor to leadership by conducting periodic, high\-level reviews to identify recurring compliance issues, control gaps, and training needs. Synthesizes and translates fundings into recommendations for action plans, workflow improvements, and decision support. Identifies patterns that warrant leadership attention, escalation, or targeted training. Provides clear guidance to support sound financial decision\-making including but not limited to financial reconciliation and reporting, budget forecasting, risk management, and award closeout. * Serves as the College’s central escalation and advisory resource for complex, high\-risk, or non\-routine post\-award financial and compliance issues, acting as liaison with SPA, the college’s pre\-award office (SciRS), and central administrative offices as needed. Addresses matters that rise beyond the departmental level, supporting the Senior Assistant Dean of F\&A in meeting accountability expectations from LSU central administration. Partners with leadership and departments to resolve audit findings and review questions. Proactively addresses grants with low expenditures nearing expiration by partnering with departments to discuss spending strategies or realignment, including payroll cost transfers and no\-cost extension considerations, without assuming departmental execution Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree * 5 years professional Qualifications** * Master's degree in finance, accounting or related discipline * 7 years of professional Requirements** Physical ability and eye\-hand coordination necessary to use a computer/ laptop/iPad; ability to sit and/or stand in the same position for extended periods (3\.5 to 4 hours) at a time. Ability to communicate clearly and effectively through the use of phone, e\-mail, Zoom/Teams, or direct contact with individuals (including conducting presentations or trainings and interacting with students/families). Overtime may be required during peak season of academic year. **Additional Job Description:** ------------------------------- **Special Instructions:** ------------------------- Please provide resume, cover letter, and three professional references including name, title, phone number and e\-mail address. Please attach ALL required documents under the "Resume/CV" section of your application. A copy of your transcript(s) may be attached to your application (if available); however, original transcripts are required prior to hire. **Posting Date:** ----------------- April 29, 2026 **Closing Date (Open Until Filled if No Date Specified):** ---------------------------------------------------------- May 13, 2026 **Additional Position Information:** ------------------------------------ **Background Check \-** An offer of employment is contingent on a satisfactory pre\-employment background check. **
sections.experience: experienced and collaborative finance professional to provide college\-level leadership in financial oversight, strategic advising, compliance, training, and process improvement in close partnership with, or in support of the Senior Assistant Dean of Finance \& Administration (F\&A). The position supports the Senior Assistant Dean of F\&A by working closely with college leadership, academic units, and central administrative offices to support sound financial stewardship and informed decision\-making. This role is designed for a seasoned financial and post\-award professional who thrives in an advisory capacity and enjoys partnering with leadership, strengthening internal controls, developing staff expertise, and resolving complex financial challenges across a distinct financial portfolio including both unrestricted and grant funds. The position provides advanced oversight and guidance for both unrestricted funds and externally sponsored awards, with a strong emphasis on compliance, risk mitigation, operational efficiency, and continuous improvement. This role blends high\-level financial analysis with hands\-on collaboration and training, serving as a trusted advisor and escalation resource for complex or high\-risk financial matters, and making recommendations on strategic and sensitive matters requiring leadership review. This position plays a critical role in strengthening the College’s financial and post\-award environment by promoting consistent practices, developing clear guidance and tools, and fostering a culture of accountability, transparency, and operational excellence. **Job experience with financial and budgetary matters including grant\-related activities and/or policy interpretation, application, or development and experience in managing projects within teams of 6\-10 individuals. **Preferred experience with financial and budgetary matters including grant\-related activities and/or policy interpretation, application, or development and experience in managing projects within teams of 6\-10 individuals. **Job Competencies** Advanced proficiency in Financial Planning \& Analysis; Post\-award; Process Improvement \& Oversight; Communication, Collaboration; Decision\-making \& Autonomy; Strategic Thinking \& Oversight; Attention to Detail; Policy Interpretation, Appliance \& Compliance; Training **Additional
